trajectory.description: Observation: baseline rmse_z500=719.63 (val 725.9) after 3 epochs, small model (~850k params), high train stride=6, loss plateaus quickly. Analysis: sufficient for pipeline validation but under-capacity and under-trained (limited data seen, no scheduler). Decision: incrementally scale model (hidden_channels=128, 8 resblocks ~2.5M params), reduce train stride to 4 (more data), increase to 15 epochs with CosineAnnealingLR (lr=5e-4), set val stride=1 for better monitoring; this should drive lower RMSE while keeping iter time ~30-60s.

trajectory.description: Observation: rmse_z500 improved to 600.5 (val 604.3) with 15 epochs on 128ch/8block ResNet (~2.4M params), loss drops fast to ~0.05. Analysis: under-capacity for dynamics, no longitude periodicity (global field), can scale safely. Decision: add PeriodicConv2d (circular pad on lon), scale to 160ch/10blocks (~5M params), train 30 epochs (lr=3e-4, wd=1e-5, Cosine T=30), batch predict.py to avoid OOM; submit eval on new best.pt.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training crashed with shape mismatch (24 vs 28 at dim 2) in residual add due to input_conv (k=5) shrinking H from 32->28 without lat padding, then resblocks shrinking further to 24; predict.py also has incorrect broadcast reshape(1,2,1,1) for 5D test inputs. Previous best 600.5 on ~2.4M model. Analysis: PeriodicConv2d only padded lon (correct for periodic), but must also pad lat (with replicate for stability) to preserve H=32 exactly; this matches common practice in global weather CNNs. Decision: Fix PeriodicConv2d to pad both dims (replicate lat, circular lon), correct norm reshape in predict.py, reduce to 20 epochs (per >20-epoch rule), keep scaled model (160ch/10blk ~5M params), lr=3e-4 cosine; run train then submit_eval on fixed best.pt for new baseline.

[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'rmse_z500': 456.7354, 'rmse_t850': 2.3878}

trajectory.description: Observation: rmse_z500 improved to 542.52 (val 544.2) after fixing padding (replicate lat + circular lon), scaling to 160ch/10blk ResNet (~5M params), 20 epochs, stride=4; loss drops fast to ~0.05 then plateaus, steady val gains. Analysis: model respects periodicity now but loss (uniform MSE on norm data) not aligned with lat-weighted Z500 RMSE metric; under-using train data (stride=4); capacity can scale further without OOM. Decision: incremental update - add lat-weighted MSE loss (cos(lat) weights applied to both channels) to align train/val, increase to hidden=192/num_blocks=12 (~9M params), stride=2 (more data), bs_train=48, epochs=25 (with grad clip, lr=2.5e-4, cosine T=25); update model default, keep predict.py; this should yield lower RMSE while measuring ~30-40min iter time.

trajectory.description: Observation: rmse_z500 improved to 456.74 (val 459.16) with ~9M param ResNet (h=192/b=12), lat-weighted MSE, stride=2, 25 epochs; loss drops to ~0.02 immediately then plateaus but val RMSE keeps improving slowly to end. Analysis: capacity still limits capturing full atmospheric dynamics (fast convergence suggests underparameterized for 3-day forecast); larger model can help without changing other hyperparameters much. Decision: scale to h=256/blocks=12 (~15M params, still <<1B), adjust lr=1.5e-4, bs_train=32 (to fit in VRAM), epochs=20 (per >20-epoch rule + time budget), update model defaults/calls in model.py/train.py/predict.py; this should yield further RMSE drop while keeping iter ~1.5-2h within remaining ~3.6h budget; submit eval after.
